stemHtml: Why is strict adherence to flight director guidance important when manually flying a radius to fix (RF) leg? The flight director…

options.a: is required to provide the necessary level of guidance and precision during the turn.
options.b: must be used in place of the autopilot as it provides more precise guidance.
options.c: provides guidance based on a fixed radius calculated with current TAS and bank angle.
options.d: allows the pilot to cross-check the DME arc guidance along the RF procedure.
